prompt.content:
---
module: reconcile_derive
deps: [drift, prompts, text_diff]
targets: [rust]
tests:
  - name: only hand-edited files that still have on-disk content are editable, in order
    given:
      files:
        - { file: "a.ts", reason: edited, current: "a" }
        - { file: "b.ts", reason: missing, current: null }
        - { file: "c.ts", reason: edited, current: null }
        - { file: "d.ts", reason: edited, current: "d" }
    expect: { editable: ["a.ts", "d.ts"] }
  - name: a reconcile file uses the recorded diff when one is present
    given:
      files:
        - { file: "a.ts", reason: edited, current: "hand edit", diff: "PRERECORDED DIFF" }
    expect: { reconcile_files: [{ file: "a.ts", diff: "PRERECORDED DIFF" }] }
  - name: a reconcile file with no recorded diff falls back to an added-from-empty diff of current content
    given:
      files:
        - { file: "a.ts", reason: edited, current: "new content", diff: null }
    expect: { reconcile_diff: "unified_diff(\"\", \"new content\")" }
  - name: deleted and content-less files produce no reconcile files
    given:
      files:
        - { file: "gone.ts", reason: missing, current: null }
        - { file: "empty.ts", reason: edited, current: null }
    expect: { reconcile_files: [] }
---
# Deriving reconcile inputs from drifted files

This module is the **pure** derivation core of the CLI's reconcile command: given
the drifted files a module's drift detection already produced, it decides which
files can be folded back into the prompt and builds the per-file inputs the
reconcile task needs. It is pure — no filesystem access, no process spawning. The
I/O shell detects drift, calls these functions over the already-read drift data,
then runs the coding agent and journals the result.

## Where this code lives

The working directory is a Cargo workspace whose root manifest is written and
owned by the toolchain — leave it alone. Create this module as its own member
crate in a subdirectory named `reconcile_derive/`: `reconcile_derive/Cargo.toml`
(package name `reconcile_derive`) and `reconcile_derive/src/lib.rs`. Touch nothing
outside `reconcile_derive/`. Ensure `cargo test` passes from the workspace root
before finishing.

## Builds on the `drift`, `prompts`, and `text_diff` modules of this workspace

This module composes on three **generated sibling member crates** in the same
workspace — not on crates.io dependencies and not on any hand-written crate. Add
path dependencies on them in your `Cargo.toml`:

- `drift` lives at `../drift`. Use its public `DriftedFile` struct and its public
  `DriftReason` enum. `DriftReason` has variants `Edited` (a file changed by hand)
  and `Missing` (a locked file deleted). `DriftedFile` has fields including `file:
  String` (path relative to the project root), `reason: DriftReason`, `current:
  Option<String>` (the current on-disk content if present), and `diff:
  Option<String>` (the baseline-to-current unified diff if one was computed).
- `prompts` lives at `../prompts`. Use its public `ReconcileFile` struct, which
  has public fields `file: String` and `diff: String`. Do not reimplement it.
- `text_diff` lives at `../text_diff`. Use its public `unified_diff(before: &str,
  after: &str) -> String` function to build a diff. Do not reimplement it.

Depending on a sibling by path only reads it; leave all three sibling crates
untouched.

## `editable_drifted(files: &[DriftedFile]) -> Vec<&DriftedFile>`

Return references to the drifted files that a reconcile can fold back into the
prompt: keep exactly those whose `reason` is `Edited` **and** whose `current`
content is `Some`, preserving the original order of `files`. Drop every `Missing`
file (it was deleted, so there is nothing to accept as a new baseline) and every
`Edited` file whose `current` is `None` (its content is not available). For a
list `[a.ts (edited, current), b.ts (missing), c.ts (edited, no current), d.ts
(edited, current)]`, the editable files are `[a.ts, d.ts]` in that order.

## `build_reconcile_files(files: &[DriftedFile]) -> Vec<ReconcileFile>`

Build one `ReconcileFile` for each editable file (using the same editability rule
as `editable_drifted`, in the same order). For each editable file:

- `file` is the drifted file's `file`.
- `diff` is the drifted file's recorded `diff` when it is `Some` (used verbatim);
  otherwise it is `unified_diff("", current)` where `current` is the file's
  current content (treat a `None` current as the empty string — though an editable
  file always has `Some` current).

So an editable file carrying a recorded diff `"PRERECORDED DIFF"` yields a
`ReconcileFile { file, diff: "PRERECORDED DIFF" }`, and an editable file with no
recorded diff and current content `"new content"` yields a `ReconcileFile` whose
`diff` equals `unified_diff("", "new content")`. Deleted or content-less files
contribute no `ReconcileFile`, so a list containing only a `Missing` file and an
`Edited` file with no current content yields an empty vector.

This module is the **pure** derivation core of the CLI's reconcile command: given

(package name `reconcile_derive`) and `reconcile_derive/src/lib.rs`. Touch nothing
outside `reconcile_derive/`. Ensure `cargo test` passes from the workspace root
before finishing.

mapl.message: No changes made — existing reconcile_derive crate already matches the spec exactly.
mapl.reasoning: The coding agent reported NO CHANGES. Per its final message the crate already exists as a workspace member (reconcile_derive/Cargo.toml + src/lib.rs) with path dependencies on drift, prompts, and text_diff, and both editable_drifted and build_reconcile_files already implement the specified editability rule, ordering, and diff-selection behavior. All 6 existing tests pass, so no source edits were needed to satisfy the contract.

mapl.message: The `None`-current fallback to `unified_diff("", "")` is unreachable by construction.
mapl.reasoning: The spec both restricts build_reconcile_files to editable files (which always have `current: Some`) and then describes fallback behavior for a `None` current, noting parenthetically that this never happens. This defensive branch is dead code given the editability rule; it is harmless but cannot be exercised by any valid input, so no test can meaningfully cover it.
mapl.suggestion: Clarify whether the `None`-current handling is intended purely as a defensive default (document it as unreachable) or drop it, since editable files are guaranteed to have `Some` current.

maplContent:
module: reconcile_derive
target: rust
entries:
  - promptLines:
      - 1
      - 66
    kind: no-op
    message: No changes made — existing reconcile_derive crate already matches the spec exactly.
    reasoning: The coding agent reported NO CHANGES. Per its final message the crate already exists as a workspace member (reconcile_derive/Cargo.toml + src/lib.rs) with path dependencies on drift, prompts, and text_diff, and both editable_drifted and build_reconcile_files already implement the specified editability rule, ordering, and diff-selection behavior. All 6 existing tests pass, so no source edits were needed to satisfy the contract.
  - promptLines:
      - 56
      - 58
    kind: note
    message: The `None`-current fallback to `unified_diff("", "")` is unreachable by construction.
    reasoning: "The spec both restricts build_reconcile_files to editable files (which always have `current: Some`) and then describes fallback behavior for a `None` current, noting parenthetically that this never happens. This defensive branch is dead code given the editability rule; it is harmless but cannot be exercised by any valid input, so no test can meaningfully cover it."
    suggestion: Clarify whether the `None`-current handling is intended purely as a defensive default (document it as unreachable) or drop it, since editable files are guaranteed to have `Some` current.

Per its final message the crate already exists as a workspace member (reconcile_derive/Cargo.toml + src/lib.rs) with path dependencies on drift, prompts, and text_diff, and both editable_drifted and build_reconcile_files already implement the specified editability rule, ordering, and diff-selection behavior. entry.message: The `None`-current fallback to `unified_diff("", "")` is unreachable by construction.
entry.reasoning: The spec both restricts build_reconcile_files to editable files (which always have `current: Some`) and then describes fallback behavior for a `None` current, noting parenthetically that this never happens. This defensive branch is dead code given the editability rule; it is harmless but cannot be exercised by any valid input, so no test can meaningfully cover it.
entry.suggestion: Clarify whether the `None`-current handling is intended purely as a defensive default (document it as unreachable) or drop it, since editable files are guaranteed to have `Some` current.
